name: Almaraz to Puerto de Campillo — circular via Puerto de Miravete and Deleitosa
name_original: Almaraz a Puerto de Campillo — circular por el Puerto de Miravete y Deleitosa
region_intro: Road cycling around Almaraz is characterized by routes traversing rolling terrain and significant water bodies. The region features open countryside, with many road cycling routes passing by the Valdecañas and Arrocampo Reservoirs. Cyclists encounter varied gradients, including ascents to mountain passes such as Puerto de Campillo and Puerto de Miravete, which provide elevation challenges. The landscape offers a mix of rural roads and scenic views across the Extremaduran plains. Best road cycling routes around Almaraz The most popular road cycling route is Almaraz to Puerto de Campillo — circular via Puerto de Miravete and Deleitosa, a 40.2 miles (64.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 0 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant climbs over two mountain passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Valdecañas Reservoir Loop from Almaraz via Puerto de Campillo, a difficult 44.6 miles (71.9 km) path. This route features extensive views of the Valdecañas Reservoir and includes a challenging ascent to Puerto de Campillo. Local road cyclists also love the Belvís de Monroy to Almaraz — circular by the Embalse de Arrocampo, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail leading through the Arrocampo Reservoir area, often completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. Road cycling around Almaraz is defined by its reservoirs, rolling hills, and open countryside. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops around water bodies to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 190 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Almaraz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Almaraz?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Almaraz, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes traverse rolling terrain and often feature views of the Valdecañas and Arrocampo Reservoirs.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Almaraz?
A: Yes, Almaraz offers routes suitable for various abilities. While many routes feature varied gradients, there are 2 easy and 15 moderate routes that are less demanding. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371652528/belvis-de-monroy-to-almaraz-circular-by-the-embalse-de-arrocampo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belvís de Monroy to Almaraz — circular by the Embalse de Arrocampo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.8-mile (25.4 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 14 minutes to complete.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in Almaraz?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Almaraz features 3 difficult routes with significant elevation gains. These routes often include ascents to mountain passes like Puerto de Campillo and Puerto de Miravete. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371626413/almaraz-to-puerto-de-campillo-circular-via-puerto-de-miravete-and-deleitosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almaraz to Puerto de Campillo — circular via Puerto de Miravete and Deleitosa&lt;/a&gt;, a 40.2-mile (64.7 km) route with over 1200 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Almaraz?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Almaraz are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371596312/valdecanas-reservoir-loop-from-almaraz-via-puerto-de-campillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop from Almaraz via Puerto de Campillo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133108" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrocampo Reservoir – Granite Pillar of Almaraz loop from Almaraz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Almaraz?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Almaraz, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot in Extremadura, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winters are typically mild but can be damp.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Almaraz?
A: Road cycling routes in Almaraz offer scenic views of the Extremaduran plains and significant water bodies like the Valdecañas and Arrocampo Reservoirs. You can also encounter historical landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granite Pillar of Almaraz&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almaraz Bridge over the Tagus River&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belvís de Monroy&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or historical sites near the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region around Almaraz is rich in history and natural beauty. Cyclists can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granite Pillar of Almaraz&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of San Sebastián de Serrejón&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belvís de Monroy&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5177450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picazo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; also offers picturesque views.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Almaraz?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the diverse terrain, from rolling hills to challenging mountain passes, and the expansive views of the reservoirs and open countryside. The region's rural roads provide a peaceful cycling experience away from heavy traffic.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in Almaraz?
A: Yes, Almaraz offers several longer routes for endurance cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38275559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belvís de Monroy – Arrocampo Reservoir loop from Almaraz&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 65 miles (105 km) and features moderate difficulty, making it suitable for a full day's ride.

Q: Is parking available near the road cycling routes in Almaraz?
A: In Almaraz and surrounding villages, you can typically find public parking areas. For routes starting directly from Almaraz, parking is generally available within the town, allowing convenient access to the cycling network.

Q: Are there any hidden gem road cycling routes in Almaraz?
A: While the main routes are well-loved, exploring the smaller, less-trafficked rural roads connecting the villages around the reservoirs can reveal quieter, equally scenic cycling experiences. These often offer unique perspectives of the Extremaduran landscape.
tips: Both the views of the bridge over the river and the panoramic view you have of the Tagus when you cross it, from the siding, justify a stop on your route.
Cycle to the Miravete mountain pass. From this point you can see the ruins of the Zúñigas fortification from the 16th century. Today, eclipsed by communication antennas.

At the southeastern end of the Monfragüe National Park is the town of Deleitosa, surrounded by oak and chestnut forests. Stop by the center of town to rest, eat something and admire the church of San Juan Evangelista.

Nice climb to Puerto de Campillo, 2.75 km coming from the south side, it is not too long. The views from the top at 700 meters above sea level are gratifying. Prepare for the descent towards Campillo de Deleitosa.

Almaraz is located near the Tagus River and the Almaraz reservoir. The Iglesia San Andrés dominates the horizon and together with the medieval Castle of Almaraz they represent the rich heritage of Alamaraz. Today, Almaraz is best known for its nuclear power plant.
